title: Weak Demand Pushes Stellantis to End Plugâ€‘In Hybrid Sales in the U.S.
content: Stellantis said it will stop selling plugâ€‘in hybrid versions of the Jeep Wrangler, Grand Cherokee and Chrysler Pacifica in the United States as it responds to soft demand and adjusts its North American electrification plans. The company will phase out plugâ€‘in hybrid programs starting with the 2026 model year and instead focus on more competitive electrified options, including hybrid and rangeâ€‘extended vehicles. The move comes as Detroit automakers reassess EV spending amid policy shifts and after Stellantis recalled about 375,000 Jeep plugâ€‘in hybrids over batteryâ€‘related fire concerns.
author: Analysts Account
